title: statice limonium subsp. angustifolia
intro: Statice limonium subsp. angustifolia, also known as Sea Lavender, is a perennial herb native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its small, white or pink flowers and narrow leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, making it a potential treatment for wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. The medicinal properties of Sea Lavender are said to have several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, fighting infections, and promoting relaxation. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as in topical applications such as creams and salves.
